users.shareWithUser: Share these with the user. Each code can only be used once.
users.deleteMessage: Delete user "{{username}}"? This cannot be undone.

security.changePasswordHelp: After changing your password, all other sessions will be signed out.
security.recoveryCodesHelp: Single-use codes to regain access if you lose your password. Regenerating replaces all existing codes.
security.saveCodesNow: Save these codes now. They will not be shown again.
security.regenerateMessage: This will invalidate all your existing recovery codes. Make sure you save the new ones.
security.passkeysHelp: Sign in with biometrics or a hardware security key.

ticketing.title: Ticketing
ticketing.description: Choose where Takuto reads work items from, and connect your personal Jira account.
ticketing.jiraPartial: Fill in the Jira site, email, and API token — or clear them to keep your current Jira connection.
ticketing.adminOnly: Only an admin can change the deployment's ticketing system. Ask an admin, or skip this step.

jiraErrors.invalid_token: Jira rejected the token — check the site URL, email, and API token.
jiraErrors.unauthorized: Your Jira account isn't authorized for that site.
jiraErrors.site_too_long: That site URL is too long.
jiraErrors.site_invalid: Enter a full site URL starting with https://.
jiraErrors.email_invalid: Enter the email tied to your Atlassian account.
jiraErrors.jira_transport_error: Couldn't reach Jira — check the site URL and your network.
jiraErrors.master_key_unavailable: The server can't seal credentials right now. Try again later.
jiraErrors.database_unavailable: The server is unavailable right now. Try again later.
jiraErrors.seal_failed: The server couldn't store the credential. Try again.
jiraErrors.write_failed: The server couldn't store the credential. Try again.

git.adminOnly: Only an admin can change the git settings. Ask an admin, or skip this step.

worktreeSettings.title: Repository Settings
worktreeSettings.description: Per-repository init commands (run when the item's container is brought up — before agent steps, the IDE, the terminal, or a run command use it) and run commands (buttons shown on your completed work item cards). These settings are private to your user account — every user manages their own.
worktreeSettings.commandsDeleted: Commands deleted. Future work items on this repository will run no init commands and show no run-command buttons.
worktreeSettings.selectRepo: Select a repository to configure its commands.
worktreeSettings.noRowHint: No commands set for this repository. Workflows here will run no init commands and show no run-command buttons. Add commands below to customize.
worktreeSettings.initCommandsHelp: Run sequentially in the worktree each time the item's container starts — before agent steps, the IDE, the terminal, or a run command use it.
worktreeSettings.runCommandsHelp: Buttons shown on your completed work item cards.
worktreeSettings.deleteConfirmMessage: Remove your init commands and run commands for "{{name}}"? Future work items on this repository will run no init commands and show no run-command buttons until you add them again.

runList.empty: No run commands. The work item card will show no run-command buttons.
runList.name: Name
runList.commandPlaceholder: e.g. cd ui && npm run dev

ai.persistWarning: AI provider settings applied in memory but NOT persisted to disk: {{reason}}. The change will be lost on next restart — fix the config volume and save again.
ai.providerSettingsHelp: Admin-only. Pick the active AI provider, configure its sub-table, and choose which providers users can pick from.

form.modelDefaultPlaceholder: Leave empty for the vendor default
form.opencodeModelHelp: Required. The model id served by your self-hosted endpoint (e.g. lmstudio/qwen3-coder or ollama/llama3).
form.privacyModeHelp: Keep your code private — never stored or indexed on Cursor's servers (recommended). Turning this off enables server-side codebase indexing (sends code to Cursor).
form.baseUrlDefaultPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the vendor public API
form.cursorNoBaseUrl: Cursor CLI does not support custom upstream endpoints.
form.opencodeBaseUrlHelp: Required. OpenCode is the self-hosted adapter — point this at your OpenAI-compatible model server. Examples: http://lm-studio:1234/v1 (LM Studio), http://ollama:11434/v1 (Ollama). To use Anthropic / OpenAI directly, pick the Claude or Codex provider instead.
form.tokenLimitsHelp: Tokens. Tells OpenCode the window of your self-hosted model so it tracks remaining context (it can't look this up for a local endpoint). Leave blank to let OpenCode choose. Match your server's loaded context length (e.g. LM Studio's per-model setting).
form.allowSharedDefaultHelp: When on, users without their own credential fall back to the deployment-default token configured in takuto.env. Default off.
form.opencodeRequires: OpenCode requires both a Base URL and a Model to save.

switch.title: Switch AI provider?
switch.body: Per-user credentials for {{from}} will be deactivated. Each user must connect their {{to}} account before they can run new workflows. Workflows already running will finish on {{from}}.
switch.migrateNote: Per-user credentials will be migrated in a later phase.

share.title: Share one conversation across a flow's steps
share.help: When on, each step resumes the previous step's session, so the agent carries full context forward — it remembers what it implemented when it reviews. When off, every step runs in a fresh session with no memory of earlier steps (the default; safer for smaller local models that get confused by long transcripts). Applies to all providers.

guardrails.title: Step guardrails
guardrails.help: Admin-only. Runtime limits applied to every agent step: how long a step may run, how long the “improve description” call may run, and the no-progress loop guard.
guardrails.stepTimeoutHelp: Maximum wall-clock time for a single agent or command step before it is aborted.
guardrails.improveTimeoutHelp: Time budget for the one-shot “improve description” helper used on the manual paste-description modal.
guardrails.maxRepeatedLinesHelp: Consecutive identical output lines that trip the no-progress guard and fail the step. 0 = off.
guardrails.persistWarning: Step guardrails applied in memory but NOT persisted to disk: {{reason}}. The change will be lost on next restart — fix the config volume and save again.

polling.title: Item polling
polling.help: Admin-only. Control which polled work items become workflows: the flow they auto-start, how many run in parallel, and the Jira / GitHub filters.
polling.persistWarning: Item polling settings applied in memory but NOT persisted to disk: {{reason}}. The change will be lost on next restart — fix the config volume and save again.
polling.enableHelp: When off, the poller is paused and no items are auto-added. Takes effect immediately and persists across restarts.
polling.intervalHelp: How often the poller checks for new work items. Minimum 10.
polling.autoStartHelp: The single flow each polled item auto-starts. Leave on the default to start every dependency-free flow.
polling.maxParallelHelp: Cap on items occupying a slot at once. 0 = unlimited.
polling.perUserHelp: When off, the cap is global across all users.
polling.issueTypesHelp: Issue types the poller pulls. Empty = no type filter.
polling.keywordsHelp: Case-insensitive substring match (ANY). Empty = no filter.
polling.labelsHelp: Exact label membership (ANY). Empty = no filter.
polling.noTicketing: No ticketing system is configured, so the poller is idle and item filters don't apply. Choose a ticketing system (Jira or GitHub) to filter polled items.

general.title: General limits
general.maxConcurrentManualHelp: Manual starts that may occupy a slot at once. 0 = unlimited.
general.prMergeIntervalHelp: How often Takuto checks GitHub for whether a workflow's PR has been merged.
general.logRetentionHelp: Days of per-work-item logs to keep. 0 = keep forever.
general.generateReportHelp: Default for whether each workflow produces an end-of-run report.

jiraContext.title: Jira context
jiraContext.linkedItemsHelp: How linked Jira issues are embedded in {{token}}.
jiraContext.ticketCapHelp: Byte cap on the main ticket description in context. 0 = unlimited.
jiraContext.linkedCapHelp: Byte cap on each linked-issue description. 0 = unlimited.
jiraContext.jqlHelp: Extra JQL appended to the poller query. Leave empty for no extra filter.
jiraContext.doneStatusHelp: Jira status the “Mark as Done” transition targets.
jiraContext.projectKeysHelp: Jira project keys the poller pulls from. Empty = no project filter.

flows.title: Workflows
flows.description: Workflows are per repository. Click one on a work-item card to run its steps in order; dependencies require an upstream workflow to have completed at least once on that work item.
flows.capTooltip: You've reached the 20-workflow limit for this repository.
flows.selectRepo: Select a repository to configure its workflows.
flows.heading: Workflows — <mono>{{repo}}</mono>
flows.emptyTitle: You have no workflows configured for <mono>{{repo}}</mono>.
flows.emptyBody: Work-item cards in this repository will show an empty state until you add at least one.
flows.deleteMessage: Delete workflow "{{name}}"? This removes it from every work-item card in this repository.
flows.reseedMessage: This replaces all workflows for {{repo}} with the defaults shipped with Takuto. Your current workflows for this repository will be lost. Other repositories are unaffected.

editor.nameNeedsAlnum: A workflow name must contain at least one letter or number.
editor.slugCollision: This name collides with "{{name}}" — both become "{{slug}}".
editor.cycle: These dependencies create a cycle involving "{{name}}". Remove one link to save.

repositories.title: My Repositories
repositories.description: Repositories you've added show up on your dashboard. Available repositories come from the deployment's GitHub App installation (or fallback PAT). The on-disk clone is shared between every user that adds the same repo.
repositories.forcePurgeMessage_one: Force-purge "{{name}}": all {{count}} associated user will lose access, and the on-disk clone at {{path}} will be deleted. This cannot be undone.
repositories.forcePurgeMessage_other: Force-purge "{{name}}": all {{count}} associated users will lose access, and the on-disk clone at {{path}} will be deleted. This cannot be undone.
repositories.lastUserMessage: You are the last user associated with "{{name}}". Removing it will also delete the on-disk clone at {{path}}. The repository can be re-added later (it will be re-cloned from the remote).
repositories.removeMessage_one: Remove "{{name}}" from your dashboard. {{count}} other user still has it added — the on-disk clone will be kept.
repositories.removeMessage_other: Remove "{{name}}" from your dashboard. {{count}} other users still have it added — the on-disk clone will be kept.

mine.empty: You haven't added any repositories yet. Pick one from the list below.
mine.forcePurgeTitle: Admin: drop the repository for every user and purge the on-disk clone.

available.noMatch: No repositories matching "{{search}}" available to add.
available.none: No additional repositories accessible via the configured GitHub credentials.

sidebar.commandsSetTitle: You have commands set for this repository
sidebar.commandsNoneTitle: No commands set for this repository
sidebar.noAccessTitle: The connected GitHub App no longer has access to this repository

commands.empty: No commands. Workspace bootstrap will skip directly to agent steps.

report.title: Generate work-item reports
report.help: When on, each flow appends its own section to this workspace's work-item report (<mono>{{file}}</mono>); re-running a flow replaces only that flow's section. A "Show Report" button then appears on completed cards.
